info.title: Postrun API
info.description:
The posting + ads API for apps and AI agents.

## Authentication

Every request carries `Authorization: Bearer <credential>`. There are two kinds:

- **Secret API key** (`pr_…`) — full account access. Use it only from your
  **backend**; never expose it to a browser.
- **Frontend token** — a short-lived, profile-scoped JWT your backend mints with
  `POST /v1/tokens` (from a secret key) and hands to the browser. The browser then
  calls this API **directly**, with no proxy backend of your own. A token grants
  only the `scopes` and profiles you mint into it.

Endpoints that accept a frontend token list **both** credentials under
*Authorizations* and say so in their description; the rest are secret-key only.
Money and structural writes are intentionally secret-key only — they can never be
reached with a browser token.

## Rate limits

Limits are **per account** (shared across all API keys and frontend tokens),
on a sliding 1-minute window, split by method:

- **Reads** (GET, plus declared read-only POST endpoints like `/gaql` and `/google/{connection_id}/insights`): 1000 per minute
- **Writes** (mutating POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE): 100 per minute

Every response carries `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and
`X-RateLimit-Reset` (unix seconds). Exceeding a bucket returns
`429 TOO_MANY_REQUESTS` with a `Retry-After` header (seconds).

ErrorCode.title: ErrorCode
ErrorCode.description: The closed set of machine-readable Postrun error codes. Branch on this. Each links to https://docs.postrun.ai/errors/<code>.

ErrorSource.description: Structured detail from the wrapped platform: its own error code, the offending field path + value, and its request id (for that platform’s support).

kind.description: The detected asset family (image/video/gif/document). We sniff it from the uploaded bytes. null only in the brief pre-detection window while status is `uploading`; non-null once probed.

content_type.description: The detected MIME of the uploaded original (e.g. video/mp4), sniffed from the bytes. null only in the brief pre-detection window while status is `uploading`.

progress.description: In-pipeline progress: the current step (stage) and a 0–100 bar (percent). Read `status` for the outcome; `percent` can regress on a transient retry.

display_error.description: A short, friendly end-user line for `code` (render verbatim). `message` stays developer-grade; this is the display string. Size/limit codes interpolate the human cap/size.

error.description: Asset-wide failure (the original itself is unusable, so no platform can succeed); null otherwise.

format.description: MIME of the original, e.g. image/heic.

url.description: Public URL of this platform’s rendition (null until ready).

display_error.description: A short, friendly end-user line for `code` (render verbatim). `message` stays developer-grade; this is the display string. Size/limit codes interpolate the human cap/size.
A short, friendly end-user line for `code` (render verbatim). `message` stays developer-grade; this is the display string. Size/limit codes interpolate the human cap/size.

metadata.description: Customer-owned key/value context, returned on reads and filterable on list endpoints (exact match). Values are scalars: string (≤500 chars), number, or boolean. At most 50 keys; keys ≤40 chars; total serialized JSON must fit in 16 KiB. Arrays and nested objects are out of scope for v1 (kept out to keep the contract scalar + exact-match). Keep a key’s value type consistent across records — filtering matches type-exactly. Never derive auth/scopes from metadata.

profile_id.description: Id of the profile this connection belongs to.

external_account_id.description: Platform account id selected from the grant (e.g. act_123 for Meta), or null while the connection is pending account selection.

external_account_name.description: Display cache of the platform account's name, or null if unknown.

username.description: Display cache of the social account @handle, or null (ad accounts, or platforms that gate it — e.g. LinkedIn).

avatar_url.description: Display cache of the social account avatar image url, or null (ad accounts have none).

profile_url.description: Display cache of the social account canonical profile url, or null (ad accounts, or gated handles).

reauth_at.description: ISO-8601 time the OAuth grant was detected dead (reconnect needed), or null when healthy. Drives the computed `needs_reauth` status.

currency.description: ISO-4217 account currency, or null if unknown.

name.description: Display name.

description.description: Optional short note, or null if unset.

external_id.description: Your own id for this client/brand, or null if unset.

status.description: Derived rollup of the variants’ statuses (never set directly).

metadata.description: Customer-owned key/value context, returned on reads and filterable on list endpoints (exact match). Values are scalars: string (≤500 chars), number, or boolean. At most 50 keys; keys ≤40 chars; total serialized JSON must fit in 16 KiB. Arrays and nested objects are out of scope for v1 (kept out to keep the contract scalar + exact-match). Keep a key’s value type consistent across records — filtering matches type-exactly. Never derive auth/scopes from metadata.

reply_settings.description: Who may reply. Omit (or `everyone`) = anyone. `everyone` is sugar that is normalised to an omitted field.

quote_tweet_id.description: Quote a post by id. Mutually exclusive with poll/card_uri/media. Enterprise-tier at publish.

duration_minutes.description: Poll duration in minutes (5 min – 7 days).

reply_settings.description: Reply restriction for the poll post (same enum).

poll.description: Create a poll. Mutually exclusive with media, quote, and card_uri.

in_reply_to_tweet_id.description: Parent post id; required when `reply` is present.

exclude_reply_user_ids.description: User ids dropped from the auto-mention chain.

card_uri.description: Cards-API reference. Mutually exclusive with quote/poll/media.

post_type.description: The post shape, DERIVED from the media (the customer never sends it). null only while the shape is undetermined — referenced media is still being probed (kind not yet known), or the current media/settings combination has no valid shape for this platform. (A text-capable platform with zero media derives `text`, not null.)

connection_id.description: The connected account this variant publishes to. null when that connection was removed (disconnected) — the variant then fails at publish time with a `connection_removed` error; reconnect + reschedule to resume.

schedule_at.description: Per-variant schedule override (ISO-8601); null inherits the post.

anyOf.description: The live post on the platform (the platform is source of truth).

display_error.description: A short, friendly end-user line for `code` (render verbatim). `message` stays developer-grade; this is the display string.

position.description: Ordering of this media within the variant (e.g. carousel order).

alt_text_override.description: Per-variant alt text; falls back to the asset’s alt_text.

visibility.description: Audience. PUBLIC or CONNECTIONS — CONTAINER/LOGGED_IN are invalid for member posts.

source.description: Article URL. Required for an article post.

description.description: Subtext on the article card, ≤4086 characters.

thumbnail_media_id.description: Optional thumbnail, referenced by OUR media id (resolved to a urn:li:image at publish).

poll.description: A LinkedIn poll (mutually exclusive with other content).

title.description: Display title shown above the document. Required for document posts.

document.description: Document-post display options (asset rides on media[]).

disable_reshare.description: Disable resharing of this post (default false).

type.description: Whether the mentioned entity is a member or an organization.

name.description: Rendered text; must match the entity name (case-sensitive) for the link to convert.

urn.description: The mentioned entity urn (supplied by the caller).

items.description: An inline @mention of a LinkedIn member or organization.

mentions.description: Inline @mentions encoded into the commentary at publish.

schedule_at.description: Per-variant schedule override (ISO-8601); null inherits the post.

position.description: Ordering of this media within the variant (e.g. carousel order).

alt_text_override.description: Per-variant alt text; falls back to the asset’s alt_text.

content_kind.description: LinkedIn content shape, DERIVED from the media + the article/poll sub-object (never sent). null while undetermined.

link.description: URL to unfurl. A feed post needs a body (message) OR a link.

location_id.description: Facebook Page id representing a location.

x.description: Horizontal position on the image, in [0,1].

y.description: Vertical position on the image, in [0,1].

user_tags.description: Tag users on an image; x/y are fractional positions in [0,1].

share_to_feed.description: Reel also appears in the main feed.

audio_name.description: Label for the reel's original audio.

settings.description: Instagram organic post settings (feed image/carousel + reels).

media_type.description: Instagram media type (IMAGE/CAROUSEL/REELS), DERIVED from the media (never sent). null while undetermined.

privacy_level.description: Audience for the post. The per-account allowed set is returned by creator_info at publish; an out-of-set value is rejected then. Unaudited apps may only use SELF_ONLY.

video_cover_timestamp_ms.description: Video cover frame offset (ms). Video posts only.

photo_cover_index.description: Which carousel image is the cover (0-based index into the ordered image list). Photo posts only.

auto_add_music.description: Auto-add a TikTok soundtrack. Photo posts only.

is_aigc.description: Disclose AI-generated content. Video posts only.

settings.description: TikTok organic post settings (Direct Post: video + photo).

TikTokCreatorInfo.description: A TikTok creator's publish options, fetched live from TikTok. The composer renders creator.nickname + avatar, a privacy dropdown built from privacy_options (with no default), and Comment/Duet/Stitch toggles per the interaction flags (true = the interaction is ALLOWED). max_video_duration_sec caps a video's length for this account.

external_account_id.description: Platform account id to pass to the select endpoint to activate the connection (e.g. act_123456789 for Meta).

name.description: Human-readable account name, or null if the platform omits it.

currency.description: ISO-4217 account currency, or null if the platform omits it.

username.description: Social account @handle for this discoverable account, or null (ad accounts / gated handles).

avatar_url.description: Social account avatar image url, or null if the platform omits it.

profile_url.description: Social account canonical profile url, or null if the platform omits it.

external_account_id.description: The linked Instagram professional account id (the IG user id).

name.description: The Instagram account username, or null if the platform omits it.

username.description: The Instagram account @handle, or null if omitted.

avatar_url.description: The Instagram account avatar image url, or null.

profile_url.description: The Instagram account canonical profile url, or null.

instagram.description: The Instagram professional account linked to this Facebook Page, if any. Present (Facebook Pages) or absent (every other platform); null when a Page has no linked Instagram account.

DiscoverableAccount.description: An external ad account reachable through a connection’s OAuth grant, offered for selection.

hosted_connect_url.description: Ready-to-open Postrun hosted connect URL. Open it (popup or tab) to run the OAuth grant and account picker — the client never hand-assembles connect params.

connect_token.description: Short-lived scoped token (connections read/write on this profile) the hosted page uses to drive the public discover/select API.

connect_session_token.description: The raw Nango connect-session token to pass to `nango.auth()` for the embedded (no-redirect) flow. ~30-min short-lived scoped bearer — keep in client memory, never log.

provider_config_key.description: The Nango integration id for the platform, the first argument to `nango.auth()` (e.g. `facebook` for Meta, `twitter-v2` for X).

nango_host.description: The Nango host the embedded SDK constructs its client with (`new Nango({ host, connectSessionToken })`).

expires_at.description: ISO-8601 time when the session token expires.

ConnectionKind.description: Whether the connection targets an ads platform (`ads`) or a posting social network (`posting`). Computed from the platform.

ConnectionStatus.description: Lifecycle status: `pending` (no account chosen), `active` (account selected, grant healthy), or `needs_reauth` (the OAuth grant died — reconnect needed). Computed from reauth_at and external_account_id.

reply_settings.description: Who may reply. Omit (or `everyone`) = anyone. `everyone` is sugar that is normalised to an omitted field.

alt_text_override.description: Per-variant alt text; falls back to the asset's.

media.description: Ordered media references; the count drives the post-type rules.

PostVariantInput.description: A single platform variant of a post: connection, platform, body, ordered media, and native typed settings. The post SHAPE (post_type, and LinkedIn content_kind / Instagram media_type) is DERIVED from the media — never sent.

publish.description: now = publish immediately; schedule = publish at schedule_at; draft = store only.

schedule_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 publish time. Required when publish = schedule.

metadata.description: Customer-owned key/value context, returned on reads and filterable on list endpoints (exact match). Values are scalars: string (≤500 chars), number, or boolean. At most 50 keys; keys ≤40 chars; total serialized JSON must fit in 16 KiB. Arrays and nested objects are out of scope for v1 (kept out to keep the contract scalar + exact-match). Keep a key’s value type consistent across records — filtering matches type-exactly. Never derive auth/scopes from metadata.

dry_run.description: Validate the post without persisting or scheduling.

variants.description: One entry per channel; at least one.

GoogleAdsAdGroup.description: A Google Ads ad group (the `ad_group` resource): id, name, status, type, and the resource name of the parent campaign.

id.description: The composite id {ad_group_id}~{ad_id} — the bare ad id is scoped to its ad group (not globally unique), so this is the handle you pass to the pause/enable/delete endpoints.

GoogleAdsAd.description: A Google Ads ad (the `ad_group_ad` resource): id, name, creative type, serving status, and the ad group resource name it belongs to.

id.description: The composite id {ad_group_id}~{criterion_id} — the bare criterion id is not globally unique (it recurs across ad groups), so this is the handle you pass to the pause/enable/bid/delete endpoints.

GoogleAdsKeyword.description: A Google Ads keyword (an `ad_group_criterion` of type KEYWORD): criterion id, text, match type, status, whether it is a negative keyword, and the owning ad group resource name.

GoogleAdsAudience.description: A Google Ads audience available to target: a user_list (remarketing / CRM list, with its type + membership status), an audience (audience asset, with its status), or a custom_audience (interest / intent custom audience, with its status + type). Pass its `resource_name` as the user_list / audience / custom_audience of an ad-group audience criterion.

GoogleAdsReadStatusFilter.description: Filter by serving status (ENABLED / PAUSED / REMOVED). Omit to return everything except REMOVED.

GoogleAdsCampaignCriterion.description: A Google Ads campaign targeting criterion. LOCATION and LANGUAGE are fully modelled (with their geo_target_constant / language_constant, status, and negative flag); any other criterion type is returned as { type, criterion_id }.

GoogleAdsAdGroupCriterion.description: A Google Ads ad-group targeting criterion. The four demographics (AGE_RANGE, GENDER, INCOME_RANGE, PARENTAL_STATUS) and the three audience kinds (USER_LIST, AUDIENCE, CUSTOM_AUDIENCE) are fully modelled with their value, status, and negative (exclusion) flag; any other criterion type (e.g. KEYWORD) is returned as { type, criterion_id }.

GoogleAdsGeoTargetConstant.description: A Google Ads geo target constant (a targetable location): its id, resource name, name, canonical name, country code, target type (Country/Region/City/…), and status. Pass the `resource_name` (`geoTargetConstants/{id}`) — NOT the bare id — as the `geo_target_constant` of a location criterion: Google’s `LocationInfo.geo_target_constant` is a resource name.

key.description: The {_key} name referenced in a tracking template / suffix. Alphanumeric, ≤ 16 bytes, unique (case-insensitive).

GoogleAdsUrlCustomParameter.description: A Google Ads URL custom parameter ({_key} → value).

id.description: The id handle of the created resource (the bare numeric id, or the composite {ad_group_id}~{id} for an association resource such as an ad or keyword), or null on a dry run.

resource_name.description: The native Google resource name of the created resource (chain it into the next create), or null on a dry run.

dry_run.description: Whether this was a validate-only dry run.

executed.description: Whether the resource was actually created.

id.description: The id handle of the edited resource (the bare numeric id, or the composite {ad_group_id}~{id} for an association resource such as a keyword).

resource_name.description: The native Google resource name of the edited resource.

dry_run.description: Whether this was a validate-only dry run.

executed.description: Whether the change was actually applied.

id.description: The id handle of the removed resource (the bare numeric id, or the composite {ad_group_id}~{id} for an association resource such as an ad or keyword).

removed.description: Whether the resource was removed (false on a dry run — Google validated the removal without applying it).

dry_run.description: Whether this was a validate-only dry run.

executed.description: Whether the removal was actually applied.

geo_target_constant.description: The geo target constant resource name to target — the `resource_name` returned by the geo-targets search (e.g. `geoTargetConstants/2840`), NOT the bare id.

negative.description: When true, EXCLUDE this location instead of targeting it (a negative location criterion).

language_constant.description: The language constant to target, e.g. `languageConstants/1000` (English). See Google’s language codes table (developers.google.com/google-ads/api/data/codes-formats#languages) for the full id list.

GoogleAdsCreateCampaignCriterionRequest.description: Add a geo (location) or language targeting criterion to a campaign.

age_range.description: The age range to target or exclude (e.g. AGE_RANGE_25_34, or AGE_RANGE_UNDETERMINED for unknown-age users).

negative.description: When true, EXCLUDE this demographic instead of targeting it (a negative criterion). Demographics are commonly used as exclusions.

gender.description: The gender to target or exclude (MALE, FEMALE, or UNDETERMINED for unknown-gender users).

negative.description: When true, EXCLUDE this demographic instead of targeting it (a negative criterion). Demographics are commonly used as exclusions.

income_range.description: The household income range to target or exclude (e.g. INCOME_RANGE_0_50 = lowest 50%, INCOME_RANGE_90_UP = top 10%, or INCOME_RANGE_UNDETERMINED).

negative.description: When true, EXCLUDE this demographic instead of targeting it (a negative criterion). Demographics are commonly used as exclusions.

parental_status.description: The parental status to target or exclude (PARENT, NOT_A_PARENT, or UNDETERMINED).

negative.description: When true, EXCLUDE this demographic instead of targeting it (a negative criterion). Demographics are commonly used as exclusions.

user_list.description: The resource name of the userLists to target — the `resource_name` from the audiences discovery read (e.g. `customers/123/userLists/456`), NOT a bare id.

negative.description: When true, EXCLUDE this demographic instead of targeting it (a negative criterion). Demographics are commonly used as exclusions.

audience.description: The resource name of the audiences to target — the `resource_name` from the audiences discovery read (e.g. `customers/123/audiences/456`), NOT a bare id.

negative.description: When true, EXCLUDE this demographic instead of targeting it (a negative criterion). Demographics are commonly used as exclusions.

custom_audience.description: The resource name of the customAudiences to target — the `resource_name` from the audiences discovery read (e.g. `customers/123/customAudiences/456`), NOT a bare id.

negative.description: When true, EXCLUDE this demographic instead of targeting it (a negative criterion). Demographics are commonly used as exclusions.

GoogleAdsCreateAdGroupCriterionRequest.description: Add a demographic (age range, gender, income range, or parental status) or audience (user list, audience, custom audience) targeting criterion to an ad group. Set negative: true to exclude instead of target.

positive_geo_target_type.description: Who a positive location targets: PRESENCE (people in or regularly in the location) or PRESENCE_OR_INTEREST (also people interested in it).

negative_geo_target_type.description: Who an excluded location excludes: PRESENCE or PRESENCE_OR_INTEREST.

GoogleAdsGeoSettingsRequest.description: Set a campaign’s geo target type (presence vs presence-or-interest).

metrics.description: The metrics to report. At least one.

segments.description: Optional breakdowns (e.g. by date or device).

campaign_id.description: Restrict to one campaign (campaign / ad_group levels only).

ad_group_id.description: Restrict to one ad group (required at the ad / keyword levels).

id.description: The bare numeric Google Ads id of this row at its level (customer / campaign / ad-group / ad / keyword-criterion id).

name.description: The display name of this row (account / campaign / ad-group name, ad name, or keyword text). Null when the resource has no name.

parent_id.description: The immediate parent in the ad hierarchy — null at account/campaign (the tree roots), the campaign id at ad_group level, the ad-group id at ad/keyword levels. Use it to attach this row to its parent when assembling the campaign→ad_group→ad/keyword tree. The bare numeric id.

metrics.description: The requested metrics keyed by name; only the metrics you asked for are present.

segments.description: The requested breakdowns keyed by segment name; present only when segments were requested.

GoogleAdsInsights.description: Performance rows for the requested level, metrics, and window.

GoogleAdsInsightLevel.description: Aggregation level: the connected account, a campaign, an ad group, a single ad, or a single keyword.

id.description: Opaque globally-unique id for this node — the row key, the parent_id link target, and the action handle. For a keyword/ad node it is the composite {ad_group_id}~{id} you pass to the keyword/ad endpoints; for a campaign/ad_group, the bare id.

name.description: The display name of this node (campaign / ad-group name, ad name, or keyword text). Null when the resource has no name.

parent_id.description: The immediate parent — null at the campaign level (the tree root), the campaign id at ad_group level, the ad-group id at ad/keyword levels. Use it to attach this node to its parent when assembling the tree.

status.description: The serving status of this node (ENABLED / PAUSED / REMOVED — Google may also return the UNSPECIFIED/UNKNOWN sentinels).

type.description: The advertising channel type — present on campaign nodes only (null at ad_group / ad / keyword).

child_kind.description: What kind of children this node has: ad_group, asset_group, or none. The per-type expand hint.

expandable.description: Whether the tree read can fetch this node’s children today (true only for ad-group channel types in this version).

budget_micros.description: The campaign’s average daily budget in micros (1/1,000,000 of the account currency). Present on campaign nodes only — null at ad_group / ad / keyword (and null when a campaign has no budget).

metrics.description: The requested metrics keyed by name; only the metrics you asked for are present. Google’s authoritative per-level numbers (no client roll-up).

cost_micros.description: Spend for this node in micros (1/1,000,000 of the account currency), as Google reports it.

cost.description: Spend for this node in the account currency (cost_micros / 1,000,000).

GoogleAdTreeNode.description: One flat node in a Google Ads campaign → ad_group → ad/keyword tree, with native structure (status, type, child_kind, expandable) and Google’s authoritative per-level metrics. Group nodes by parent_id to assemble the tree.

GoogleAdTree.description: Flat tree nodes for the requested level or subtree and window. Group by parent_id to assemble the tree.

ad_user_data.description: Consent for Google to use the data for ad measurement (CONSENT_GRANTED / CONSENT_DENIED).

ad_personalization.description: Consent for Google to use the data for ad personalization (CONSENT_GRANTED / CONSENT_DENIED).

GoogleAdsConversionConsent.description: EEA/UK consent signals. Set at the request level as a default; a per-event value overrides it.

email_address.description: A plaintext email address. Normalized + hashed server-side.

phone_number.description: A plaintext phone number (E.164 preferred). Normalized + hashed server-side.

address.description: A plaintext postal address — all four parts are required. Name fields are hashed; region/postal code are sent in the clear.

GoogleAdsConversionUserIdentifier.description: A plaintext customer identifier (email, phone, or address) used to match the conversion. Hashed server-side.

event_timestamp.description: When the conversion happened, as an RFC 3339 timestamp (required). A UTC `Z` or an explicit timezone offset (e.g. +05:30) is accepted.

event_source.description: Where the conversion happened (required by Google): WEB for a gclid web conversion, or APP / IN_STORE / PHONE / MESSAGE / OTHER.

order_id.description: Your unique transaction id for this conversion (Google’s transaction_id). Used to de-duplicate.

gclid.description: The Google click id from the ad click (a match signal).

gbraid.description: The click id for an iOS app-to-web journey (a match signal).

wbraid.description: The click id for an iOS web-to-app journey (a match signal).

user_data.description: Plaintext customer identifiers (1–10) used to match the conversion. Hashed server-side.

currency.description: The ISO 4217 currency code (3 letters) for conversion_value.

consent.description: Per-event consent; overrides the request-level consent.

GoogleAdsConversionEvent.description: A single conversion to send. Requires an event_timestamp and at least one match signal (gclid/gbraid/wbraid or user_data).

request_id.description: Google’s request id for this batch (poll the status read with it), or null on a dry run.

status.description: accepted = the batch was queued for async processing; validated = a dry run passed validation.

dry_run.description: Whether this was a validate-only dry run.

executed.description: Whether the events were actually sent.

reason.description: Google’s raw processing-error reason (descriptive; branch on `code`, not this).

count.description: How many events failed for this reason.

code.description: The typed Postrun error code to branch on.

display_error.description: A short, friendly line you may show your end user.

count.description: How many events triggered this warning.

request_id.description: The request id this status is for.

request_status.description: The destination’s processing status (SUCCESS / PROCESSING / FAILED / PARTIAL_SUCCESS).

events_submitted.description: The total events Google received for this request, INCLUDING failures (null while not yet known).

destinations.description: One entry per destination, ordered to match the request’s destinations. EMPTY for the first ~minute after a send, while Google registers the request — poll again until it populates.

request_id.description: Google’s request id for this send. Pass it to the status read for the per-destination processing result.

conversion_action_id.description: The conversion action id you targeted in this send (null for sends made before this was recorded).

event_count.description: How many conversion events you submitted in this send (null for sends made before this was recorded).

secretKey.description: Secret API key (`Authorization: Bearer pr_…`). Backend only — never expose it to a browser.

frontendToken.description: Short-lived, profile-scoped frontend token minted via `POST /v1/tokens`. Browser-safe; grants only the scopes and profiles it was minted with.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Optional. A unique key (1–255 chars; a UUID is ideal) that makes this write safe to retry: the first request executes and its outcome is stored; an identical retry replays that exact outcome instead of re-executing. A retry with the same key but a different body returns 422; a retry while the first is still in flight returns 409. See https://docs.postrun.ai/idempotency.

post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `profiles:write` with account-wide profile scope (`all`); a profile-scoped token cannot create.

Creates a profile — a client/brand workspace under your account, and the unit connections attach to.

name.description: Display name for the profile (your client or brand).

description.description: Optional short note about the profile (a couple of sentences).

external_id.description: Your own id for this client/brand. Unique within your account — set it to look the profile up later and avoid a join table on your side.

metadata.description: Your own key/value context, returned on reads.

get.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `profiles:read` (the response is limited to the profiles the token is scoped to).

Lists the profiles under your account, newest first.

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return (1–100). Defaults to 20.
Number of items to skip before this page. Defaults to 0.
Return only the profile with this exact external_id (your own client/brand id).

Filter by metadata key/value pairs (exact match; multiple keys are ANDed; omit for no filter). REST: a URL-encoded JSON object, e.g. ?metadata=%7B%22campaign%22%3A%22summer%22%2C%22priority%22%3A3%7D. SDK/MCP: pass the object directly. Values are scalars (string/number/boolean), matched type-exactly. No operators or ranges.

total.description: Total items matching the query, across all pages.

limit.description: The limit applied to this page.

offset.description: The offset applied to this page.

has_more.description: True if more items exist after this page.

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return (1–100). Defaults to 20.

patch.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `connections:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Activates a pending connection by choosing which external account (e.g. which Meta ad account) it uses. The account must be one reachable through the connection’s OAuth grant; its canonical name + currency are resolved server-side from discovery, so the stored display values can’t be spoofed by the caller.

external_account_id.description: The external account to activate this connection with (e.g. act_123 for Meta).

delete.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `connections:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Disconnects a connection. Returns a deletion marker; returns 404 if it does not exist in your account. Disconnect is unconditional — any scheduled posts that target this connection are not blocked; they fail gracefully at publish time with a `connection_removed` error until you reconnect and reschedule.

post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `media:write` scoped to the target profile.

Creates a media asset. Two ways to supply the bytes: (1) omit `source_url` and upload the bytes with the returned multipart `upload` session — chunk the file at `part_size`, sign each part via `POST /media/{id}/multipart/sign`, PUT the bytes, then `POST /media/{id}/multipart/complete` (the direct-upload path; `upload` is non-null and resumable), or (2) supply a public `https` `source_url` and we fetch the bytes server-side, validate, transform, and store them (the import path; `upload` is null). Either way the asset settles through the same probe/transform flow.

profile_id.description: The profile that owns this asset. A profile is one brand/client; media is profile-owned so a scoped browser token stays safely confined to that brand — it can create/read media only under the profiles its token is scoped to, never reach another client’s assets. This is the tenancy boundary of the frontend-token model, so it is always required (it’s never inferable from the bytes).

source_url.description: A public https URL to import the bytes from. When supplied, we fetch (SSRF-guarded), validate, transform, and store the media server-side — no signed upload target is returned (`upload` is null). Omit it to PUT the bytes to the signed upload target instead. Size limits match a direct upload: a video may be up to 4 GB, every other kind up to 1 GB (a larger source is rejected with `media_too_large`).

targets.description: The targets to render for: the post platforms (x / linkedin / facebook_page / instagram) plus the ad target `google_ads`. We validate + render a per-target rendition for each. The targeted set IS what is resolved (omit to target none yet; a later PATCH can add more). When `raw` is true we still validate against these targets — we just never transform the bytes.

raw.description: false (default) = process to platform-ready (validate + transform). true = validate against the targets but NEVER transform and NEVER block on MEDIA validation: every targeted platform gets a ready entry on the ORIGINAL url, with any "would have resized/converted/rejected" surfaced as a non-blocking finding in `warnings`. The platform is the final judge. (The one boundary raw does NOT relax: `google_ads` is image-only, so once byte-detection finds the asset is not an image, that target is rejected with a typed `media_unsupported` failure — a target-kind error, not a media-quality verdict.)

metadata.description: Customer-owned key/value context, returned on reads and filterable on list endpoints (exact match). Values are scalars: string (≤500 chars), number, or boolean. At most 50 keys; keys ≤40 chars; total serialized JSON must fit in 16 KiB. Arrays and nested objects are out of scope for v1 (kept out to keep the contract scalar + exact-match). Keep a key’s value type consistent across records — filtering matches type-exactly. Never derive auth/scopes from metadata.

schema.description: Create a media asset — either via the signed direct upload target (omit source_url) or by importing from a public https source_url (we fetch + validate + transform + store). Size limits (same for direct upload and source_url): a video may be up to 4 GB; every other kind must be 1 GB or smaller. A file over its limit is rejected with `media_too_large`.

kind.description: The detected asset family (image/video/gif/document). We sniff it from the uploaded bytes. null only in the brief pre-detection window while status is `uploading`; non-null once probed.

content_type.description: The detected MIME of the uploaded original (e.g. video/mp4), sniffed from the bytes. null only in the brief pre-detection window while status is `uploading`.

progress.description: In-pipeline progress: the current step (stage) and a 0–100 bar (percent). Read `status` for the outcome; `percent` can regress on a transient retry.

display_error.description: A short, friendly end-user line for `code` (render verbatim). `message` stays developer-grade; this is the display string. Size/limit codes interpolate the human cap/size.

error.description: Asset-wide failure (the original itself is unusable, so no platform can succeed); null otherwise.

format.description: MIME of the original, e.g. image/heic.

url.description: Public URL of this platform’s rendition (null until ready).

display_error.description: A short, friendly end-user line for `code` (render verbatim). `message` stays developer-grade; this is the display string. Size/limit codes interpolate the human cap/size.
A short, friendly end-user line for `code` (render verbatim). `message` stays developer-grade; this is the display string. Size/limit codes interpolate the human cap/size.

metadata.description: Customer-owned key/value context, returned on reads and filterable on list endpoints (exact match). Values are scalars: string (≤500 chars), number, or boolean. At most 50 keys; keys ≤40 chars; total serialized JSON must fit in 16 KiB. Arrays and nested objects are out of scope for v1 (kept out to keep the contract scalar + exact-match). Keep a key’s value type consistent across records — filtering matches type-exactly. Never derive auth/scopes from metadata.

upload_id.description: The R2 multipart upload id. Echoed for your reference; the sub-endpoints read it from the asset, so you never pass it back.

key.description: The R2 object key the parts assemble into (for your logs).

part_size.description: Chunk the file at exactly this many bytes. Every part except the last MUST be this size (an R2 requirement); the last part is whatever remains.

expires_at.description: ISO-8601 expiry of the signed part URLs.

anyOf.description: A resilient multipart upload session. Upload the file in `part_size` chunks to presigned part URLs (from `/multipart/sign`), then `/multipart/complete`. Resume after a drop via `/multipart/parts`. No Content-Type is required — we detect the type from the bytes.

get.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `media:read` (the response is limited to the profiles the token is scoped to).

Lists the media assets under your account, newest first. Filter by profile, status, kind, your own external_id, or metadata.

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return (1–100). Defaults to 20.
Number of items to skip before this page. Defaults to 0.
Restrict to media owned by a single profile.
Only assets in this lifecycle state (uploading / processing / ready / failed).
Only assets of this family (image / video / gif / document). Unset on assets still in the pre-detection `uploading` window, so a `kind` filter excludes those.
Look up the asset by your own id (exact match).

Filter by metadata key/value pairs (exact match; multiple keys are ANDed; omit for no filter). REST: a URL-encoded JSON object, e.g. ?metadata=%7B%22campaign%22%3A%22summer%22%2C%22priority%22%3A3%7D. SDK/MCP: pass the object directly. Values are scalars (string/number/boolean), matched type-exactly. No operators or ranges.

total.description: Total items matching the query, across all pages.

limit.description: The limit applied to this page.

offset.description: The offset applied to this page.

has_more.description: True if more items exist after this page.

patch.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `media:write` scoped to the profile that owns the media asset.

Updates a media asset (alt text, metadata, external_id) or adds `targets` to pre-warm more platforms. Send only the fields to change.

metadata.description: Customer-owned key/value context, returned on reads and filterable on list endpoints (exact match). Values are scalars: string (≤500 chars), number, or boolean. At most 50 keys; keys ≤40 chars; total serialized JSON must fit in 16 KiB. Arrays and nested objects are out of scope for v1 (kept out to keep the contract scalar + exact-match). Keep a key’s value type consistent across records — filtering matches type-exactly. Never derive auth/scopes from metadata.

targets.description: Extend the targeted set with these targets (post platforms or `google_ads`). A ready asset resolves them immediately from its stored source; an un-probed one resolves them on the next read. Already-targeted targets are unaffected.

post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `posts:write` scoped to the target profile.

Composes a post + its per-channel variants. `publish` decides the outcome: `now` publishes immediately, `schedule` publishes at `schedule_at`, `draft` just stores it.

status.description: Derived rollup of the variants’ statuses (never set directly).

metadata.description: Customer-owned key/value context, returned on reads and filterable on list endpoints (exact match). Values are scalars: string (≤500 chars), number, or boolean. At most 50 keys; keys ≤40 chars; total serialized JSON must fit in 16 KiB. Arrays and nested objects are out of scope for v1 (kept out to keep the contract scalar + exact-match). Keep a key’s value type consistent across records — filtering matches type-exactly. Never derive auth/scopes from metadata.

dry_run.description: Whether this was a validate-only dry run.

executed.description: Whether the post was actually persisted (false on a dry run).

reply.in_reply_to_tweet_id: Example in reply to tweet id

get.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `posts:read` (the response is limited to the profiles the token is scoped to).

Lists the posts under your account, newest first. Filter by profile or your own external_id.

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return (1–100). Defaults to 20.
Number of items to skip before this page. Defaults to 0.
Restrict to posts for a single profile.
Look up the post by your own id (exact match).

Filter by metadata key/value pairs (exact match; multiple keys are ANDed; omit for no filter). REST: a URL-encoded JSON object, e.g. ?metadata=%7B%22campaign%22%3A%22summer%22%2C%22priority%22%3A3%7D. SDK/MCP: pass the object directly. Values are scalars (string/number/boolean), matched type-exactly. No operators or ranges.
Only posts scheduled at or after this ISO-8601 time (inclusive, on schedule_at). Posts with no schedule_at (unscheduled drafts / publish-now) are excluded from any date-bounded query.
Only posts scheduled at or before this ISO-8601 time (inclusive, on schedule_at). Posts with no schedule_at (unscheduled drafts / publish-now) are excluded from any date-bounded query.

Only posts changed at or after this ISO-8601 time (inclusive) — the cheap delta cursor for live-status polling. "Changed" tracks BOTH envelope edits and variant status transitions (publish lifecycle), via greatest(post.updated_at, max(variant.updated_at)). Pass the timestamp from your previous poll to fetch only what moved since. A post with no recorded change time is excluded (NULL >= x is NULL in SQL).
Only posts whose derived status is one of these. Repeat the param or pass an array to filter on multiple (e.g. `?status=scheduled&status=failed`).

total.description: Total items matching the query, across all pages.

limit.description: The limit applied to this page.

offset.description: The offset applied to this page.

has_more.description: True if more items exist after this page.

patch.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `posts:write` scoped to the profile that owns the post.

Edits a post and/or transitions its publish state (`draft` / `schedule` / `now`). Valid only while the post is draft or scheduled. Send only the fields to change; providing `variants` replaces the whole set.

metadata.description: Customer-owned key/value context, returned on reads and filterable on list endpoints (exact match). Values are scalars: string (≤500 chars), number, or boolean. At most 50 keys; keys ≤40 chars; total serialized JSON must fit in 16 KiB. Arrays and nested objects are out of scope for v1 (kept out to keep the contract scalar + exact-match). Keep a key’s value type consistent across records — filtering matches type-exactly. Never derive auth/scopes from metadata.

status.description: Derived rollup of the variants’ statuses (never set directly).

metadata.description: Customer-owned key/value context, returned on reads and filterable on list endpoints (exact match). Values are scalars: string (≤500 chars), number, or boolean. At most 50 keys; keys ≤40 chars; total serialized JSON must fit in 16 KiB. Arrays and nested objects are out of scope for v1 (kept out to keep the contract scalar + exact-match). Keep a key’s value type consistent across records — filtering matches type-exactly. Never derive auth/scopes from metadata.

dry_run.description: Whether this was a validate-only dry run.

executed.description: Whether the post was actually persisted (false on a dry run).

post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `posts:read` scoped to the target profile.

Runs the exact ownership + publish-readiness checks the create path runs under dry_run, with no side effects. Returns a per-issue verdict (`publishable` + `issues[]`) on 200; a reference to a connection/media outside the profile is a typed 422.

variants.description: One entry per channel; at least one.

platform.description: The platform this issue belongs to. Group/branch on this directly rather than re-deriving from `variant_index` (variant order is canonical, not your channel order).

display_error.description: Short, friendly, render-this end-user line (the Plaid display_message model). The `code` stays the branch key for customer branching; `message` stays developer-grade. Populated centrally — never at construction sites.

get.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:read` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Retrieves the Meta ad account this connection is linked to — name, currency, status, and spend. Monetary fields are in the account currency’s minor units.

schema.description: The connection (a linked Meta ad account) to act on.

id.description: Ad account id in `act_<ID>` form (the connection anchor).

name.description: Account display name, or null if unset.

account_status.description: Meta account status code (1 = ACTIVE, 2 = DISABLED, …).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code — the unit for every monetary field here.

amount_spent.description: Current amount spent by the account, in `currency` minor units (string).

balance.description: Current account balance, in `currency` minor units (string).

spend_cap.description: Account spend cap in `currency` minor units, or null if uncapped.

get.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:read` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Retrieves performance insights for the connection’s Meta ad account, aggregated at the chosen `level` over a date window. Metric values are strings, in the account currency’s units.

schema.description: The connection (a linked Meta ad account) to act on.
Aggregation level — `account` totals, or per-`campaign`/`adset`/`ad` rows.
Relative date window. Ignored when `time_range` is provided.
Explicit inclusive date range (YYYY-MM-DD). Takes precedence over `date_preset`.
Days per row (1–90), `monthly`, or `all_days` (default: one aggregated row).

cpc.description: Cost per click, in account-currency units.

schema.description: A list of curated Meta ad-insights rows.

get.summary: Retrieve the connected Google Ads account
get.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:read` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Returns the Google Ads account (the `customer` resource) backing this connection — id, descriptive name, currency, time zone, status, and whether it is a manager (MCC) or test account.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.

tracking_url_template.description: The account-level tracking URL template ({lpurl}/{unescapedlpurl}…), or null if unset. A campaign-level template OVERRIDES this (lowest level wins).

final_url_suffix.description: The account-level final-URL suffix (UTMs live here), e.g. "utm_source={_src}", or null if unset.

schema.description: A connected Google Ads account (the `customer` resource): id, descriptive name, currency, time zone, status, whether it is a manager (MCC) or test account, and the account-level URL tracking settings.

patch.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Updates the account-level (customer) URL tracking settings — tracking_url_template and final_url_suffix. A campaign-level template overrides the account one. Pass `dry_run: true` to validate against Google without applying.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.

tracking_url_template.description: Set the account-level tracking URL template ({lpurl}/{unescapedlpurl}…). Omit to leave unchanged; clearing is not yet supported.

final_url_suffix.description: Set the account-level final-URL suffix (UTMs live here), e.g. "utm_source={_src}". Omit to leave unchanged; clearing is not yet supported.

dry_run.description: When true, validate the request against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only).

post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:read` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Performance metrics for the connection’s account, at a chosen level (account / campaign / ad group / ad / keyword), for a date range, with optional breakdowns. Leaf levels (ad / keyword) require an `ad_group_id`; campaign / ad group accept an optional `campaign_id`. Each row carries its `parent_id`, so the tree is assemblable. The flagship cross-checked read; use `/gaql` for anything outside this shape.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.

metrics.description: The metrics to report. At least one.

segments.description: Optional breakdowns (e.g. by date or device).

campaign_id.description: Restrict to one campaign (campaign / ad_group levels only).

ad_group_id.description: Restrict to one ad group (required at the ad / keyword levels).

get.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:read` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

The campaign → ad_group → ad/keyword tree as flat, native, typed nodes — group them by `parent_id` to assemble the tree. Without `campaign_id`, returns every campaign (all channel types) with a per-type `child_kind`/`expandable` hint. With `campaign_id`, returns that campaign’s whole supported subtree (ad groups + the type’s leaf — keywords for Search, ads for Display/Video/Demand Gen) in one batched fetch at a single window. By default REMOVED nodes are hidden at every level; pass `status` to filter to specific serving states (ENABLED / PAUSED / REMOVED). Each node carries `budget_micros` (campaign nodes only), Google’s authoritative per-level metrics (no client roll-up), `cost_micros`, and `cost` (account currency).

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.
Restrict to one campaign — returns that campaign’s subtree (ad groups + leaf). Omit for the campaign level.
The metric columns to report. At least one.

post.summary: Create a Google Ads campaign budget
post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key only — this endpoint cannot be called with a browser token.

Creates a standalone daily campaign budget (the `campaign_budget` resource) on the connection’s account. Money write — secret-key-only. Pass `dry_run: true` to validate against Google without creating. Returns the new resource id + resource_name to chain into a campaign.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.

name.description: A name for the budget, unique within the Google Ads account.

amount_micros.description: The daily budget amount in micros of the account currency (1,000,000 micros = 1 unit, e.g. $1.00).

dry_run.description: When true, validate the request against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only).

post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Creates a Search or Display campaign (the `campaign` resource) on the connection’s account, attached to an existing budget. Pass `dry_run: true` to validate against Google without creating. Returns the new resource id + resource_name.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.

name.description: A name for the campaign, unique within the Google Ads account.

channel.description: The advertising channel type. Launch scope: SEARCH or DISPLAY (Performance Max is a fast-follow).

campaign_budget.description: The id of the budget to attach, as returned by a budget create (POST /budgets). The full resource name is built server-side from the connection’s account.

bidding.description: Which embedded bidding strategy to set. bidding_strategy_type is output-only and auto-derived by Google.

status.description: The campaign status. Defaults to PAUSED so a new campaign never spends by accident.

contains_eu_political_advertising.description: The campaign’s EU political-advertising declaration (Google requires it). Defaults to DOES_NOT_CONTAIN_EU_POLITICAL_ADVERTISING.

dry_run.description: When true, validate the request against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only).

patch.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Updates a campaign’s non-money fields — its name and URL tracking settings (tracking_url_template, final_url_suffix, url_custom_parameters) — by numeric id. Status has its own pause/enable endpoints; bids are a separate money write. Pass `dry_run: true` to validate against Google without applying.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.
The numeric id of the resource.

name.description: A new name for the campaign, unique within the Google Ads account.

tracking_url_template.description: Set the tracking URL template ({lpurl}/{unescapedlpurl}…). A campaign-level template OVERRIDES the account-level one. Omit to leave unchanged; clearing is not yet supported.

final_url_suffix.description: Set the final-URL suffix (UTMs live here), e.g. "utm_source={_src}". Omit to leave unchanged; clearing is not yet supported.

url_custom_parameters.description: Replace the campaign’s URL custom parameters (the whole list, up to 8 {_key} → value substitutions). Omit to leave unchanged; clearing is not yet supported.

dry_run.description: When true, validate the request against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only).

get.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:read` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Resolves a free-text location name (e.g. "New York") to matching geo target constants — each with id, name, canonical name, country code, target type, and status. Use a result’s `resource_name` (e.g. `geoTargetConstants/2840`), NOT the bare `id`, as the `geo_target_constant` of a location criterion.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.
A free-text location name to resolve (e.g. "New York", "California", "United Kingdom").
Optional ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 country code to bias the search (e.g. "US").

post.summary: Add a Google Ads campaign targeting criterion
post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Adds a geo (location) or language targeting criterion to a campaign. Pass `dry_run: true` to validate against Google without creating. Returns the new resource id + resource_name.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.
The numeric id of the campaign.

dry_run.description: When true, validate the request against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only).

geo_target_constant.description: The geo target constant resource name to target — the `resource_name` returned by the geo-targets search (e.g. `geoTargetConstants/2840`), NOT the bare id.

negative.description: When true, EXCLUDE this location instead of targeting it (a negative location criterion).

language_constant.description: The language constant to target, e.g. `languageConstants/1000` (English). See Google’s language codes table (developers.google.com/google-ads/api/data/codes-formats#languages) for the full id list.

patch.summary: Set a Google Ads campaign’s geo target type
patch.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Sets a campaign’s geo target type — presence (people in the location) vs presence-or-interest (also people interested in it) — for positive and/or excluded locations. Pass `dry_run: true` to validate without applying.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.
The numeric id of the campaign.

dry_run.description: When true, validate the request against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only).

positive_geo_target_type.description: Who a positive location targets: PRESENCE (people in or regularly in the location) or PRESENCE_OR_INTEREST (also people interested in it).

negative_geo_target_type.description: Who an excluded location excludes: PRESENCE or PRESENCE_OR_INTEREST.

post.summary: Create a Google Ads ad group
post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Creates an ad group (the `ad_group` resource) under a campaign on the connection’s account. Pass `dry_run: true` to validate against Google without creating. Returns the new resource id + resource_name to chain into ads and keywords.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.

name.description: A name for the ad group, unique within the campaign.

campaign.description: The numeric id of the parent campaign, as returned by POST /campaigns.

type.description: The ad group type; must match the parent campaign channel: SEARCH_STANDARD for a Search campaign, DISPLAY_STANDARD for a Display campaign.

status.description: The initial serving status of the ad group. Defaults to ENABLED.

dry_run.description: When true, validate the request against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only).

post.summary: Add a Google Ads ad-group targeting criterion
post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Adds a demographic (age range, gender, income range, or parental status) or audience (user list, audience, or custom audience) targeting criterion to an ad group. Audience criteria reference the `resource_name` returned by the audiences discovery read (`GET .../audiences`). Set `negative: true` to exclude instead of target. Pass `dry_run: true` to validate against Google without creating. Returns the new resource id + resource_name.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.
The numeric id of the ad group.

dry_run.description: When true, validate the request against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only).

age_range.description: The age range to target or exclude (e.g. AGE_RANGE_25_34, or AGE_RANGE_UNDETERMINED for unknown-age users).

negative.description: When true, EXCLUDE this demographic instead of targeting it (a negative criterion). Demographics are commonly used as exclusions.

gender.description: The gender to target or exclude (MALE, FEMALE, or UNDETERMINED for unknown-gender users).

income_range.description: The household income range to target or exclude (e.g. INCOME_RANGE_0_50 = lowest 50%, INCOME_RANGE_90_UP = top 10%, or INCOME_RANGE_UNDETERMINED).

parental_status.description: The parental status to target or exclude (PARENT, NOT_A_PARENT, or UNDETERMINED).

user_list.description: The resource name of the userLists to target — the `resource_name` from the audiences discovery read (e.g. `customers/123/userLists/456`), NOT a bare id.

audience.description: The resource name of the audiences to target — the `resource_name` from the audiences discovery read (e.g. `customers/123/audiences/456`), NOT a bare id.

custom_audience.description: The resource name of the customAudiences to target — the `resource_name` from the audiences discovery read (e.g. `customers/123/customAudiences/456`), NOT a bare id.

patch.summary: Set a Google Ads campaign’s targeting setting
patch.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Sets a campaign’s targeting setting for one dimension (e.g. AUDIENCE): Observation (`bid_only: true` — does NOT narrow reach) vs Targeting (`bid_only: false` — narrows reach to the targeted criteria). Campaign + dimension scoped: shared by every audience criterion in the campaign. Pass `dry_run: true` to validate without applying. Note: Google rejects setting this on a campaign whose ad group already has a targeting setting (and vice versa).

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.
The numeric id of the campaign.

dry_run.description: When true, validate the request against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only).

targeting_dimension.description: The criteria dimension this setting controls — most often AUDIENCE.

bid_only.description: true = Observation (the criteria do NOT narrow reach; report/bid only). false = Targeting (narrows reach to the targeted criteria). Default in Google is Targeting; Observation is recommended for audience segments in Search.

post.summary: Create a Google Ads responsive display ad
post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Creates a Responsive Display Ad (the `ad_group_ad` resource) under a Display ad group on the connection’s account, assembled from already-uploaded image-asset resource_names (upload them first via POST /v1/google/{connection_id}/assets). Pass `dry_run: true` to validate against Google without creating. Returns the new resource id + resource_name.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.

ad_group.description: The numeric id of the ad group this ad belongs to.

final_urls.description: The landing page URLs for the ad (at least one).

asset.description: The resource_name of an uploaded Google Ads image asset.

marketing_images.description: Landscape (1.91:1) marketing image asset resource_names. Combined with square_marketing_images: at least 1, at most 15.

asset.description: The resource_name of an uploaded Google Ads image asset.

square_marketing_images.description: Square (1:1) marketing image asset resource_names. Combined with marketing_images: at least 1, at most 15.

asset.description: The resource_name of an uploaded Google Ads image asset.

logo_images.description: Landscape (4:1) logo image asset resource_names. Combined with square_logo_images: at most 5.

asset.description: The resource_name of an uploaded Google Ads image asset.

square_logo_images.description: Square (1:1) logo image asset resource_names. Combined with logo_images: at most 5.

text.description: Short headline text, up to 30 characters.
The long headline text, up to 90 characters.

long_headline.description: The required long headline (up to 90 characters).

business_name.description: The advertiser/business name, up to 25 characters.

main_color.description: Optional main brand color as a hex string (e.g. #0a0a0a).

accent_color.description: Optional accent brand color as a hex string (e.g. #ffffff).

get.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:read` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Lists the keywords (`ad_group_criterion` of type KEYWORD) in the connection’s account — each with its criterion id, text, match type, status, negative flag, and owning ad group. Pass `ad_group_id` to return only the keywords under one ad group. By default REMOVED keywords are hidden; pass `status` to filter to specific serving states (ENABLED / PAUSED / REMOVED).

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.
Only return keywords under this ad group (its numeric id).

post.summary: Create a Google Ads conversion action
post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Creates a conversion action (the `conversion_action` resource) — a website tag (WEBPAGE) or an offline/API-reported action (UPLOAD_CLICKS) — on the connection’s account. Pass `dry_run: true` to validate against Google without creating. Returns the new resource id + resource_name.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.

name.description: A name for the conversion action, unique within the Google Ads account.

type.description: The conversion action type. Launch scope: WEBPAGE (a website tag) or UPLOAD_CLICKS (offline / API-reported conversions).

category.description: The conversion category (e.g. PURCHASE, SIGNUP, SUBMIT_LEAD_FORM) — any member of Google’s ConversionActionCategory.

status.description: The conversion action status. Only ENABLED is a valid create state (the proto has no PAUSED); defaults to ENABLED.

default_value.description: The value to assign a conversion when the event reports no value.

default_currency_code.description: The ISO 4217 currency code (3 letters) for the default value.

always_use_default_value.description: When true, always use the default value (ignore any value reported with the event).

value_settings.description: How to value conversions. Omit to let Google apply its category-based defaults.

patch.summary: Update a Google Ads conversion action
patch.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Updates the mutable fields of a conversion action (name, category, status, value settings, counting type, lookback windows, primary-for-goal) by numeric id. Only the supplied fields change. Pass `dry_run: true` to validate against Google without applying.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.
The numeric id of the resource.

name.description: A name for the conversion action, unique within the Google Ads account.

category.description: The conversion category (e.g. PURCHASE, SIGNUP, SUBMIT_LEAD_FORM) — any member of Google’s ConversionActionCategory.

status.description: The conversion action status. Only ENABLED is settable (the proto has no PAUSED). Use the remove endpoint to soft-delete.

default_value.description: The value to assign a conversion when the event reports no value.

default_currency_code.description: The ISO 4217 currency code (3 letters) for the default value.

always_use_default_value.description: When true, always use the default value (ignore any value reported with the event).

value_settings.description: How to value conversions. Omit to let Google apply its category-based defaults.

counting_type.description: How to count conversions per ad interaction: ONE_PER_CLICK (e.g. leads) or MANY_PER_CLICK (e.g. purchases).

click_through_lookback_window_days.description: The max days between an ad click and a conversion to attribute it (click-through).

view_through_lookback_window_days.description: The max days between an ad impression and a conversion to attribute it (view-through).

primary_for_goal.description: Whether this action is a primary (biddable) action for its conversion goal. Pass false to make it observation-only. Update-only: on create Google always starts an action as primary.

dry_run.description: When true, validate the request against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only).

post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Sends a batch of conversion events (1–2000) for one conversion action on the connection’s account, via the Data Manager API. Plaintext identifiers (email / phone / address) are hashed server-side. Pass `dry_run: true` to validate against Google without sending. Returns a request id; poll the status read with it for per-reason processing results.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.

conversion_action_id.description: The numeric id of the conversion action these events count toward.

conversions.description: The conversion events to send (1–2000 per request).

consent.description: Request-level consent default, applied to every event without its own consent.

dry_run.description: When true, validate the request against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only).

get.summary: Retrieve Google Ads conversion send status
get.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:read` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Returns the per-destination, count-by-reason processing status for a prior conversion send (by its `request_id`) — the total events submitted, the error breakdown (with typed codes), and informational warnings. `destinations` is EMPTY for the first ~minute after a send (Google registers the request asynchronously) — poll until it populates.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.

post.summary: Set a Google Ads conversion goal’s biddable flag
post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Sets whether a conversion goal (selected by category × origin) is biddable — a primary goal Google optimises toward, or a secondary observation-only goal. Pass `dry_run: true` to preview without applying. Returns the goal and whether it executed.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.

category.description: The conversion category of the goal to update.

origin.description: The origin of the goal to update.

biddable.description: Whether the goal is biddable: true makes it a primary goal Google optimises toward, false makes it secondary (observation-only).

dry_run.description: When true, validate the request against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only).

schema.description: The outcome of a conversion-goal update: the goal’s category and origin, the new biddable value, whether it was a dry run, and whether it executed.

post.summary: Upload a Google Ads image asset
post.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `ads:write` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Uploads a Postrun image asset (its validated Google Ads rendition) to the connection’s account as an `image` asset, returning the new resource id + resource_name to chain into an ad/asset assembly. Pass `dry_run: true` to validate against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only). The media asset must have a ready Google Ads rendition, else a 409.

schema.description: The id of a Google Ads connection in your account.

media_id.description: The id of a Postrun media asset (its validated Google Ads rendition is uploaded).

dry_run.description: When true, validate the request against Google without creating anything (Google’s native validate_only).

get.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key, or a browser-safe frontend token granting `connections:read` scoped to the profile that owns the connection.

Retrieves the TikTok creator's publish options live from TikTok — required by TikTok's Content Posting policy to render the publishing UI: the creator nickname + avatar, the privacy options to offer (with no default), and the Comment/Duet/Stitch flags to disable. Returns 404 if the connection does not exist in your account or is not a TikTok connection, 409 if the TikTok account must be reconnected, and 502 if TikTok’s creator-info call fails.

get.description:
**Authentication.** Secret `pr_` API key only — this endpoint cannot be called with a browser token.

Lists the API activity for your account — one entry per action, newest first. Filter by profile, connection, platform, status, source, action, or time range.

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return (1–100). Defaults to 20.
Number of items to skip before this page. Defaults to 0.
Only logs for this connected account.
Only logs for this exact action (e.g. `post.publish`).
Only logs created at or after this ISO-8601 time.
Only logs created at or before this ISO-8601 time.

source.description: Origin of the action: `api` (your integration), `dashboard` (you), `mcp` (an agent), or `scheduler` (a scheduled job).

status.description: Outcome: `pending` (async result not in yet), `success`, or `error`.

status_code.description: HTTP status, or null while pending.

platform.description: Platform the action targeted, or null.

request_method.description: HTTP method of the originating request, or null.

request_path.description: Path of the originating request, or null.

request_body.description: Redacted request payload (never secrets), or null. Always null for Google Ads actions — request/response payloads are not retained for Google Ads (privacy / Google API Limited Use: no stored copy of Google Ads data).

response_body.description: Redacted response payload, or null. Always null for Google Ads actions — request/response payloads are not retained for Google Ads (privacy / Google API Limited Use: no stored copy of Google Ads data).

code.description: Stable, machine-readable error code from the closed registry.

error.description: Typed error `{ code, message }` for a failed action, or null on success. INVARIANT: `status: "error"` always carries a non-null `error`. For a publish that failed/partially-published it is the rollup summary (`post_publish_failed` / `post_partially_published`), distinct from any single target — the per-target reasons live in `outcome.targets[].error`. Stored as jsonb.

status.description: Rolled-up status of the publish (published | partially_published | failed | …), derived from the targets.

connection_id.description: The connected account this variant targets. null when that connection was removed before publish.

code.description: The typed error code the variant carries.

display_error.description: A short, friendly end-user line for `code` (render verbatim). `message` stays developer-grade.

targets.description: One entry per platform target: its status, live post id, permalink, and typed error.

anyOf.description: Per-target breakdown of a publish action: the rolled-up status plus one entry per platform target (live post id, permalink, typed per-target error). Identical vocabulary to the post.completed webhook.

outcome.description: Per-target publish breakdown (see LogOutcome): the rollup status plus one entry per platform target. Present on a publish action (create/update/scheduled publish that ran), null otherwise.

external_id.description: Platform resource id the action produced (e.g. a post id).

idempotency_key.description: Idempotency-Key the caller sent, or null.

duration_ms.description: End-to-end latency in milliseconds, or null.

profile_id.description: Profile the action belongs to, or null.

connection_id.description: Connected account the action targeted, or null.

api_key_id.description: API key that made the call, or null.

total.description: Total items matching the query, across all pages.

limit.description: The limit applied to this page.

offset.description: The offset applied to this page.

has_more.description: True if more items exist after this page.
